data.title: Manchester United Q1 loss narrows, tightens FY25 sales outlook
data.description:
Driven by a 50% increase in matchday revenue thanks to 4 extra home games and robust ticket sales on the team’s appearance in the Europa League finals, Manchester United (NYSE:MANU) trimmed its quarterly loss and tightened its FY25 revenue guidance, lifting shares more than 6% ahead of Friday’s open.

With higher ticket sales, the increase in matchday revenue, along with a 10.1% gain in broadcasting revenue and 7.3% increase in commercial revenue all contributed to a 17.4% increase in total revenue for the first quarter to £160.5M ($217.5M), beating  expectations of £151.9.

And although operating expenses were up 20% during the quarter due to more home games as well as the additional costs associated with the new e-commerce model, the company’s loss narrowed to £0.03 ($0.04) per share, improving from a loss of £0.24 a year ago and better than estimates for a loss of £0.24 per share.

For FY25, Manchester United (NYSE:MANU) tightened its revenue guidance to £660M to £670M from earlier guidance of £650M to £670M, above £632.13M estimates, and raised its adjusted EBITDA guidance to be between £180 million and £190 million from £145M to £160M, previously.

data.title: SanDisk Corporation prices upsized secondary offering at $38.50 per share
Mosaic slumps after cutting Q2 phosphates sales volume forecast
data.description:
Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) -5.4% pre-market Friday after lowering guidance for Q2 and FY 2025 phosphate sales volumes while raising its outlook for phosphate prices.

The company said it expects Q2 phosphate sales volumes at 1.5M-1.6M metric tons, revised down from its previous outlook of 1.7M-1.9M tons, while FY 2025 production volume is now forecast at 7M-7.3M tons, revised down from 7.2M-7.6M tons.

The company said it extended planned downtimes at its Riverview facility to eliminate bottlenecks, causing production to miss initial expectations, and discovered additional repairs were needed at its Louisiana facilities, resulting in extended outage periods and some lost production; Riverview and Lousiana are expected to achieve run rates corresponding to annual production of 1.6M tons and 1.4M tons, respectively, in Q3.

Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) said production rates have improved in the areas where work has been concluded at all phosphate facilities; as remaining maintenance and reliability enhancement work will be completed in June and July, the company continues to expect operating rates to achieve the 8M-tons target run rate across the full portfolio of U.S. phosphate assets.

Phosphate price guidance moves higher, with DAP prices on an FOB basis expected at $650-$670/ton in Q2, up from previous guidance of $635-$655/ton, reflecting strong market conditions.

In potash, Q2 sales volumes are expected at 2.3M-2.5M tons, unchanged from prior guidance, with prices expected to remain stable at MOP prices of $230-$250/ton; for the full year, production volumes guidance remains unchanged at 9.0M-9.4M tons.

data.title: ClearBridge Energy Midstream Opportunity Fund reports $904.1M in net assets as of May 31
data.description: ClearBridge Energy Midstream Opportunity Fund (NYSE:EMO) Friday said that as of May 31, the Fund’s net assets were $904.1 million, and its net asset value per share was $49.70.

data.title: Melcor Developments announces normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Melcor Developments (TSX:MRD:CA) received TSX approval to renew its normal course issuer bid from June 10, 2025, to June 9, 2026.
  • Melcor may repurchase up to 1.51M common shares, about 5% of its outstanding shares.
  • Under the previous normal course issuer bid, Melcor repurchased 344,248 shares at an average price of $12.59.
  • All repurchased shares will be cancelled.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Douglas Dynamics declares $0.295 dividend
data.description:
• Douglas Dynamics (NYSE:PLOW) declares $0.295/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.29%
  • Payable June 30; for shareholders of record June 17; ex-div June 17.
  • See PLOW Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Logistic Properties files for potential public offering of common stock
data.description:
Logistic Properties of the Americas (NYSE:LPA) confidentially filed a draft registration statement for a potential public offering of its common stock in "the U.S. and elsewhere".

The industrial and logistics real estate company filed the statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Form F-1.

The offering size, pricing, and timing remain undecided and subject to market conditions.

Shares were -0.13% Friday pre-market to $7.84.

data.title: Ryvyl completes debt-to-equity conversion
data.description:
Fintech Ryvyl (NASDAQ:RVYL) announced on Friday the conversion of its remaining principal outstanding on the 8% senior convertible note into shares of common stock.

Shares were -9.87% pre-market to $0.81.

The entire outstanding principal balance of $4.0M and accrued interest of $136,000 has been converted into 7.1M shares.

"We've pursued an anti-dilutive strategy to restructure our balance sheet, and this final debt-to-equity conversion successfully completes that process," said CFO George Oliva.

data.title: Broadcom in focus as AI growth continues to impress
data.description:
Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) was in the spotlight on Friday after the semiconductor and software company reported stronger-than-expected second-quarter results and guidance, leading to much praise from analysts and investors. 

Shares fell 2.2% in premarket trading, while other semiconductor stocks, such as Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD) and Marvell (MRVL) moved fractionally.

Lucas Ma of Envision Research, Investing Group Leader for Envision Early Retirement, said he was “particularly optimistic” about Broadcom's differentiated accelerator products and its customer base. “Most of its customers are hyperscalers with a large internal installed base to potentially deploy AVGO’s next-gen accelerators,” Ma said via email. 

Jonathan Weber, Investing Group Leader for Cash Flow Club, said he saw “compelling” growth, thanks to AI data center spending. He also pointed out strength in the infrastructure software business and the continued rise in margins, thanks to operating leverage.

Jefferies analyst Blayne Curtis said the custom silicon business is likely to ramp up in the second half of the year, aided by the next generation of Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) tensor process units. 

“The ASIC business is still expected to have 3 customers deploying 1M XPU clusters in 2027, but with added upside potential from inference buildouts in 2H26,” Curtis, who has a Buy rating and $300 price target on Broadcom, wrote in a note to clients. 

“Performance remained on track through the transition periods, with growth expected to accelerate with the Ironwood TPU (v7p) ramp in 2H. All in, the AI segment is expected to continue growing at the current pace through the rest of FY25 and FY26 (~60% growth, including the Q3 AI guide).”

Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore slightly raised his price target — going to $270 from $260 — and kept his Overweight rating on Broadcom, citing the positive commentary for 2026.

“Company highlights potential to maintain 2025 growth rates in CY26,” Moore wrote in a note to clients.

Moore continued: “We don't want to be overly literal about a qualitative comment before 2025 is over, but that implies $26-30B in AI revenue, higher than what is in Street models. It seems consistent with the view, expressed six months ago, that there is a $60-90B [serviceable addressable market] in 2027; we certainly need to be high $20B range to get to a strong portion of that [serviceable addressable market] one year later.”

data.title: Accuray restructures debt, secures new $190M financing; shares down
data.description:
• Accuray (NASDAQ:ARAY) agreed to exchange $82M of its 3.75% convertible notes due 2026 for 8.88M shares and $68.6M in cash.
  • ARAY entered a new $190M senior secured credit facility with TCW, including a $150M term loan, $20M delayed draw loan, and $20M revolver.
  • Proceeds will fully repay prior debt with Silicon Valley Bank.
  • Accuray issued 23.4M warrants to lenders, with anti-dilution protections and long-term expiration dates.
  • TCW’s Steven F. Mayer joins Accuray’s board, with two more TCW observers added under a new governance agreement.
  • ARAY shares fall nearly 8% premarket on Friday.
  • Source: Press release
